title: cerastium vulgare var. holosteoides
intro: Cerastium vulgare var. holosteoides, also known as mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the carnation family that is native to Europe and has been naturalized in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of mouse-ear chickweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Mouse-ear chickweed can be prepared as a herbal infusion, tincture, or salve, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for a range of health issues.
